elements.title: Mount Zion Church of God in Christ
elements.contents: ... that baptisms hosted by the Mount Zion Church of God in Christ (pictured) became a tourist attraction?

elements.title: Flawed Hero: Truth, Lies and War Crimes
elements.contents: ... that the Australian War Memorial changed its rules retroactively to disqualify Flawed Hero: Truth, Lies and War Crimes from a prize it was already selected to receive?

elements.title: Anton Bonke
elements.contents: ... that basketball player Anton Bonke represented his home nation, Vanuatu, in rowing?

elements.title: LOT Polish Airlines Flight 703
elements.contents: ... that the captain of LOT Polish Airlines Flight 703 was initially hailed as a hero before an investigation later blamed the flight crew for the crash?

elements.title: the University of Southern California's radio station
elements.contents: ... that the University of Southern California's radio station went from "rinky-dink" to a national producer of classical-music programs within a decade?

elements.title: Al Culver
elements.contents: ... that Al Culver jumped into Lake Michigan to save a drowning man at the 1933 Chicago World's Fair?

elements.title: Werdmuller Centre
elements.contents: ... that the derelict Werdmuller Centre was a filming location for The Dark Tower and the Resident Evil TV series?

elements.title: Abraham Howell
elements.contents: ... that Abraham Howell established a colony on Crab Island without government approval?

elements.title: Post Office Square
elements.contents: ... that Post Office Square does not have any post offices?
